name: Physiotherapy (musculoskeletal)
description: Refer yourself for assessment and treatment of musculoskeletal problems — back pain, joint pain, sports injuries, and more. No GP referral needed.
howToRefer: Complete the NHS Fife online self-referral form or call the physiotherapy department directly.

name: Podiatry / Foot Health
description: For conditions affecting your feet and lower limbs, including nail problems, skin conditions, and diabetes-related foot care.
howToRefer: Call the NHS Fife Podiatry advice line or complete the self-referral form. The Randolph Wemyss Memorial Hospital clinic can also be contacted directly.

name: Mental health (Access Therapies Fife)
description: Talking therapies for anxiety, low mood, depression, and stress — including CBT, counselling, and guided self-help. Free on the NHS.
howToRefer: Self-refer online, by phone, or via webchat through Access Therapies Fife. You do not need to speak to a GP first.

name: Pelvic Health Physiotherapy
description: Specialist physiotherapy for bladder, bowel, and pelvic floor problems — including incontinence, prolapse, and pelvic pain.
howToRefer: Self-refer via NHS Fife. A self-management leaflet is also available online.

name: Maternity services
description: If you think you might be pregnant and would like to book antenatal care, you can contact NHS Fife Maternity Services directly.
howToRefer: Refer yourself via the NHS Fife maternity self-referral page or contact the antenatal clinic.

name: Termination of pregnancy
description: Confidential support and referral for termination of pregnancy. You do not need to see a GP first.
howToRefer: Contact Women's Choices Scotland or NHS Fife Women's Health Unit for confidential advice and onward referral.

name: Sexual assault support (SARCS)
description: The Sexual Assault Referral Centre (SARCS) provides confidential support for anyone who has experienced sexual assault or rape — recent or historical.
howToRefer: Call the SARCS 24-hour phone line. No appointment or GP referral is needed.

name: Sexual health and contraception
description: Confidential sexual health services including STI testing, contraception advice and fitting, and treatment. No GP referral needed.
howToRefer: Contact Fife Sexual Health directly by phone or book online. Drop-in sessions are also available.

name: Fife Childhood Immunisation Service
description: For queries about childhood vaccinations, schedules, or missed doses — contact the NHS Fife Childhood Immunisation Service directly.
howToRefer: Call or email NHS Fife Childhood Immunisation Service. You do not need to go through your GP.

name: Bladder and Bowel Service (BABS)
description: Specialist support for bladder and bowel problems, including incontinence, constipation, and urgency.
howToRefer: Self-refer by phone or email to the NHS Fife Bladder and Bowel Service.

name: Virtual Hand Clinic
description: Assessment and advice for hand and wrist conditions — including fractures, tendon problems, and trigger finger — without a GP appointment.
howToRefer: Refer yourself via the Fife Virtual Hand Clinic website.

name: Smoking cessation (Quit Your Way)
description: Free NHS support to help you stop smoking, including one-to-one advice, nicotine replacement therapy, and group sessions.
howToRefer: Call Quit Your Way Fife or speak to your pharmacist. Support is free and available to everyone.

name: Working Health Services
description: Free occupational health and physiotherapy support for employed patients in Scotland whose health condition is affecting their ability to work.
howToRefer: Self-refer by phone. Available to people currently in employment — including part-time and self-employed workers.

name: Social work (Fife Council)
description: For community care, adult support, carer support, and social work assessments.
howToRefer: Contact the Fife Council Social Work contact centre directly. No GP referral is needed.

name: Fife Bus (community transport)
description: Accessible community transport for patients who have difficulty getting to medical appointments and have no other means of transport.
howToRefer: Contact Fife Council to check eligibility and arrange transport to healthcare appointments.

name: RVS Community Transport
description: Voluntary transport service helping older and disabled people get to medical and other essential appointments.
howToRefer: Contact RVS (formerly WRVS) in Fife to arrange transport. A small contribution may be requested.

name: Foot Care Fife (voluntary nail-cutting)
description: A voluntary service providing basic foot care and nail cutting for eligible patients who are unable to manage this themselves.
howToRefer: Call the free helpline to find out if you or someone you care for is eligible.

name: Travel vaccinations
description: Please note: Scoonie Medical Practice no longer provides travel health services or travel vaccinations. For travel health advice and vaccinations, please contact NHS Fife or a registered travel health clinic.
howToRefer: Contact NHS Fife travel health services or use the NHS Inform travel health guide.

name: Urgent dental care
description: For urgent dental problems, please do not attend the health centre — we are unable to treat dental conditions. Contact the Fife Dental Help Line for advice and out-of-hours access.
howToRefer: Call the Fife Dental Help Line for urgent dental problems outside normal dental practice hours.

name: Optometry (eye care)
description: For eye problems, changes to your vision, or eye health queries, contact an optician first. They can examine your eyes and refer you if needed — no GP referral required. For certain conditions, visit Eyes.Scot.
howToRefer: Book directly with any NHS-registered optician. Use Eyes.Scot for guidance on common eye conditions and headaches.
